content: The state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be repurposed to solve the core limitation of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM/RND): the 'curiosity vanishing' problem in open-ended long-horizon tasks, by selectively retaining only high-information unexpected exploratory trajectories in long-term episodic memory instead of overwriting or forgetting them. This creates a 'cumulative curiosity' loop where past exploratory novelty guides future active exploration, rather than treating curiosity as a purely intrinsic real-time reward signal.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
